title: Ireland Discovery.

trains.name: Trains & Taxi Transfers
trains.blurb: Scheduled rail between cities, private taxi transfers at each end.

transport.1: Dublin airport to Dublin hotel private transfer.
transport.3: Dublin to Limerick direct train, II Class (approx. 2h 15m).
transport.6: Limerick to Dublin train via Kilkenny connection, II Class.
transport.7: Dublin hotel to Dublin airport private transfer.

inclusions: Dublin airport–hotel private transfers on arrival and departure
Dublin to Limerick train ticket, II Class
Limerick to Dublin train ticket, II Class

selfdrive.name: Self Drive
selfdrive.blurb: Rental car from arrival to departure — explore at your own pace.

transport.1: Pick up rental car at Dublin airport — SUV class, Nissan Qashqai or similar.
transport.3: Drive Dublin → Cliffs of Moher → Limerick at your own pace (self-guided route notes provided).
transport.6: Drive Limerick → Kilkenny → Dublin. Suggested stops en route.
transport.7: Return rental car at Dublin airport before departure.

inclusions: Rental car for 7 days, SUV class Nissan Qashqai or similar
Self-guided route notes with recommended stops

private.name: Private Exclusive Coach
private.blurb: Private minivan or coach with driver — ideal for families and small groups.

transport.1: Dublin airport to Dublin hotel private transfer. Private vehicle on disposal Day 3–6.
transport.2: Dublin panoramic tour, 3 hrs with private vehicle.
transport.3: Private transfer Dublin → Cliffs of Moher → Limerick with photo stops.
transport.6: Private transfer Limerick → Kilkenny → Dublin with guided stop.
transport.7: Dublin hotel to Dublin airport private transfer.

inclusions: Private minivan on disposal from Day 3 till Day 6
Dublin airport–hotel private transfers on arrival and departure
Private Dublin – Cliffs of Moher – Limerick transfer

days.title: Arrive Dublin
days.desc: Arrive in the Irish capital and settle in. The rest of the day is free — Temple Bar, Grafton Street and the Georgian squares are all an easy walk from the city-centre hotels.
days.taste: A first pint of stout and a bowl of Irish stew at a Victorian pub — try The Long Hall, pouring since 1766.

days.title: Dublin
days.desc: Full day exploring Dublin with the Hop-On Hop-Off ticket: Trinity College and the Book of Kells, Dublin Castle, Kilmainham Gaol and the Guinness Storehouse.
days.experience: Dublin's historic pub crawl through the Temple Bar and around St. Stephen's Green — literary pubs once frequented by Joyce and Behan.

days.title: Dublin → Cliffs of Moher → Limerick
days.desc: Cross the island to the west coast. The Cliffs of Moher rise 214 m above the Atlantic — allow two hours at the visitor centre and cliff-edge paths before continuing to Limerick.
days.taste: Seafood chowder and brown soda bread in Doolin village, the traditional lunch stop before the cliffs.

days.title: Killarney day trip
days.desc: A day among the lakes and mountains of Killarney National Park — Muckross House, Torc Waterfall and Ladies View.
days.experience: A jaunting-car ride through the national park with a local jarvey — Killarney's signature since the 1800s.
days.fallbackIncluded: Day at leisure — optional Ring of Kerry tour available.

days.title: Limerick & the Shannon
days.desc: At leisure in Limerick: King John's Castle, the Milk Market (Fri–Sun) and the riverside Three Bridges walk. Bunratty Castle & Folk Park is 15 minutes away as an optional add-on.
days.taste: The Milk Market for farmhouse cheeses and blaas — soft white rolls unique to this region.

days.title: Limerick → Kilkenny → Dublin
days.desc: Return east with a stop in medieval Kilkenny — castle, Black Abbey and the Medieval Mile — before the final night in Dublin.
days.shopping: Kilkenny Design Centre in the castle stables — Irish craft, knitwear and pottery from over 200 makers.

days.title: Depart Dublin
days.desc: Free time until your transfer to Dublin airport, depending on flight time.

hotels.h3: Hampton by Hilton Dublin City Centre
Hampton by Hilton Dublin City Centre

about.title: Best season
about.body: May–September for long days; shoulder months quieter & better value

about.title: Weather
about.body: Mild & changeable, 12–20°C in summer. Pack a rain layer year-round

about.title: Currency & docs
about.body: Euro (€). Republic of Ireland — separate from UK visa area

goodToKnow.title: Tickets & timing
goodToKnow.body: Book Kilmainham Gaol and the Book of Kells ahead in summer — both sell out days in advance. Cliffs of Moher are quietest before 11:00.

goodToKnow.title: Driving
goodToKnow.body: Ireland drives on the left. West-coast roads are narrow — allow more time than the map suggests. Parking discs needed in Limerick city centre.

goodToKnow.title: Rail travel
goodToKnow.body: Seat reservations are included on inter-city trains. Dublin Heuston is the station for Limerick — 20 minutes by taxi from the city-centre hotels.

goodToKnow.title: Your vehicle
goodToKnow.body: Driver working hours are limited to 10 hrs per day by law — day-trip timings are planned around this. Luggage space fits one large case per guest.

goodToKnow.title: What to pack
goodToKnow.body: Layers and a waterproof in any month. Comfortable shoes for cobbles and cliff paths. No formal dress codes apply on this programme.

goodToKnow.title: Money
goodToKnow.body: Cards accepted almost everywhere; a little cash is useful for markets and rural pubs. Tipping 10% in restaurants is customary, not obligatory.

terms: All rates are net and per person for the package.
Child rates apply for children aged 2 to 11 years old. One child sharing a room with 2 adults, subject to hotel policy.
All rates are subject to availability at the time of booking. Europe Incoming will endeavour to match the rates quoted.
Rates are not applicable during trade fair periods, major European public holidays, and other major events.
City taxes are not included in the price.
If certain attractions are closed during specific periods, alternative options will be arranged.
All bookings must be confirmed at least 60 working days prior to arrival.
100% pre-payment required by bank transfer or credit card (Visa or Mastercard). All bank transfer charges covered by the Agent.
Vouchers will be issued after receipt of full payment.
